title: Register a CRD and create a custom resource
scenario: Your team wants to ship an "EchoOperator" that reflects a message from a CR's spec back into its status. Before the operator can watch anything, you need to register a CustomResourceDefinition for `Echo` objects in the `kubeforge.io` API group — then create a CR instance to prove the CRD is established.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: echoes.kubeforge.io
spec:
  group: kubeforge.wrong
  names:
    kind: Echo
    plural: echoes
    singular: echo
  scope: Namespaced
  versions:
  - name: v1
    served: true
    storage: true

objectives.description: CR `hello-echo` of kind `Echo` exists in the `default` namespace

hints.text: The CRD's `spec.group` must exactly match the apiVersion prefix used in your CR. `kubeforge.wrong/v1` won't match a CR using `kubeforge.io/v1`.
Fix `spec.group: kubeforge.wrong` → `kubeforge.io`. Then add a second YAML document (separated by `---`) that creates an Echo CR.

Echo CR template:
```yaml
apiVersion: kubeforge.io/v1
kind: Echo
metadata:
  name: hello-echo
  namespace: default
spec:
  message: Hello from KubeForge
```

solution:
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: echoes.kubeforge.io
spec:
  group: kubeforge.io
  names:
    kind: Echo
    plural: echoes
    singular: echo
  scope: Namespaced
  versions:
  - name: v1
    served: true
    storage: true
---
apiVersion: kubeforge.io/v1
kind: Echo
metadata:
  name: hello-echo
  namespace: default
spec:
  message: Hello from KubeForge
